_doc: THROWAWAY (ckpt 155) — dump the live KEYBIND CONFIG to resolve which ring id each key produces. Config base = *(0x8a6e80); the producer 46a880 reads a keybind ARRAY (entry stride 0x1c): {enabled @cfg+E, scancode @cfg+E+0x10} → a hardcoded ring id per slot. Read each slot's scancode (DIK) so we can map scancode->id and find Z (DIK 0x2c) / X (0x2d) / C (0x2e). chain va=0x8a6e80 hops=[0] (deref to cfg base) off=<scancode offset>. Slot->id (from 46a880): 0x558->8(+held128) 0x574->7(jump) 0x590->9(attack/X) 0x5ac->0x24/0x26(confirm) 0x5c8->0x14 0x5e4->0x10 0x600->0x15 0x61c->0xa 0x638->0x16 0x654->0x17 0x670->0xb 0x68c->0x28 0x6a8->0x29 0x6c4->0x2a 0x6e0->0x2b 0x6fc->0x18 0x718->0x19. RUN: frida_capture.py --no-turbo --lockstep --duration-ms 60000 --field-spec tools/flow/kb_config_fields.json --field-spec-only --call-trace --call-trace-frames <a few title frames> --run-dir runs/sword/kbcap.

0x5b8fc0.name: kb_config
0x5b8fc0._note: FUN_005b8fc0 Flip; read the keybind config scancodes (DIK) per slot.

fields.name: en_id24
fields.reason: cfg+0x59c enabled flag for the id-0x24 slot (sanity)
